name: Dyson Pure Cool DP04 Desk Air Purifier Fan
introText: The Dyson Pure Cool DP04 combines HEPA + Carbon air purification with a desk-sized bladeless fan — the only unit that delivers personal-space air cleaning and cooling simultaneously in a compact 61cm tower for desks and nightstands.

specs.Noise: As low as 37 dBA on lowest

whatWorks: HEPA filtration in a desk-sized form factor is a unique combination — no other compact desk fan provides true HEPA air purification. For offices, bedrooms, and nurseries where a full-size floor unit is too large, the DP04's footprint is a genuine advantage.
Bladeless Air Multiplier design is safe for desks with children or pets — no exposed blades that can cause injury from accidental contact, and easy to clean without disassembling a blade assembly.
App and remote control allows changing fan speed and oscillation without touching the unit — useful when the DP04 is mounted on a nightstand and you want to adjust settings while lying in bed.
350° programmable oscillation covers most room directions from a desk position — the programmable arc lets you focus airflow where you need it rather than oscillating past empty walls.
worthKnowing: $479 for a desk fan is a premium price — the Dyson Pure Cool DP04 is significantly more expensive than combining a small Levoit air purifier and a standard desk fan, which would cost under $150 for comparable coverage.
Filter replacement at ~$70/yr is the same ongoing cost as the HP09 and TP09 — Dyson's filter pricing doesn't scale down for smaller units.

Airflow strength is limited compared to floor tower models — the compact size produces less raw airflow volume than the Dyson TP09 or dedicated fans. For hot summer cooling, a standalone tower fan moves more air.
Oscillation range is smaller than floor models — 350° is the maximum rotation, covering most but not all room directions when placed on a desk away from walls.

commonComplaints.title: Very expensive for a desk fan…
commonComplaints.body: Very expensive for a desk fan — at $479, the DP04 costs more than a full-size floor air purifier from Coway or Levoit plus a separate fan, which would cover more area.

commonComplaints.title: Less airflow than floor models or…
commonComplaints.body: Less airflow than floor models or dedicated fans — the compact size limits the volume of air moved, making it less effective as a primary room cooling solution in hot weather.

commonComplaints.title: Filter cost same as larger units…
commonComplaints.body: Filter cost same as larger units despite smaller coverage — Dyson charges the same ~$70 per replacement filter regardless of model size, making the DP04's per-unit economics unfavourable.

verdict: The DP04 is the right product for a specific scenario: a desk or nightstand where you want both air purification and a fan, and you can't or don't want a full-size floor unit. In a nursery, a small bedroom, or a home office desk, it's excellent. As general-purpose air purification, a Levoit Core 300 + small desk fan costs one-third as much and covers the same space.

realBuyers.quote: Perfect for my baby's room. No exposed blades, genuinely quiet on low, and the air quality in the nursery is noticeably better.
Worth it for the design alone. Looks great on my desk and does what it says. No regrets.
The app control is legitimately useful for the nightstand position. I can turn it down at 2am without getting up.

sources.title: Dyson DP04 vs Levoit Core 300 — YouTube

metaDescription: Dyson Pure Cool DP04 review for Canada: HEPA + Carbon, 61cm desk size, 10 speeds, 350° oscillation, app control. ~$479 CAD on Amazon.ca.
